text.title: agents-memory-system Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for agents-memory-system. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Agents MUST summarize conversation history every N steps (N = 10-20) when running loops longer than the summarization threshold. The summary MUST preserve all task-relevant facts, active tool states, and key decisions; it MUST drop redundant exchanges and intermediate reasoning steps.
rule.rationale: Without summarization, context grows linearly with steps. At 20 steps of ReAct with verbose tool outputs, most 128K-token models hit their limit. Summarization compresses 80% of history into 10% of tokens while preserving the facts needed for the next steps.

rule.statement: Long-term memory recall MUST use a relevance threshold (cosine similarity >= 0.7 by default). The agent MUST NOT inject all stored memories into the context — only the top-k (k=3-5) memories above threshold. The recall query MUST be the current task or goal text, not a generic "what do I know" query.
rule.rationale: Injecting all memories into every prompt defeats the purpose of the memory system and can overflow the context window. Thresholded top-k recall injects only the facts that are semantically relevant to the current task, keeping the context lean.
